(2) The members of the Courier Service Licensing Committee
shall be appointed by the Minister.

sections.heading: (1) The Courier Service Licensing Committee shall regulate
sections.text:
Functions of
Committee courier service business in the Republic through the issuance oflicences
under these Regulations.
(2) The Courier Service Licensing Committee shall have the power
to issue licences to operators of courier services.
(3) The Courier Service Licensing Committee may issue a licence
to an applicant on such terms and conditions as are specified under
these Regulations and on payment of such fees as are set out in the
Third Schedule.
PARTID

sections.heading: (1) The Minister shall appoint a Registrar ofth e Courier Service
sections.text:
Registrar and
other staff Licensing Committee who shall be a public officer.
(2) The Registrar shall be responsible for-
(a ) the management and administration of the Courier Service
Licensing Committee; and \
(b) the implementation of the decisions of the Courier Service
Licensing Committee.
(3) The Minister may appoint suitable public officers to assist the
Registrar.
(4 ) Notice of the appointment of the Registrar and other public
officers to assist the Registrar shall be published in the Gazette.
Allowances of 7. There shall be paid from the funds appropriated for the functions
Committee ofth e Courier Service Licensing Committee--
(a ) such reasonable sitting and subsistence allowances for the
members of the Courier Service Licensing Committee

undertaking the business of the Committee at such rates as
the Minister may determine; and
(b) any other expenses incurred by the CourierS ervice Licensing

sections.heading: (1) Subject to the provisions of these regulations, an Application
sections.text:
for courier
application for a courier service licence shall be in form CSI set service
out in the First Schedule. licence
(2) Subject to subregulation (3) the applicantshall submit
the application form referred to in subregulation (1) which shall
be accompanied by the following documents:
(a ) the certificate of incorporation of the operator of a courier
service;
(b) statement of paid up capital;
( c) Memorandum of Association or such other company
forms as may reveal the true nature and identity of the
't
company and its shareholders;
(d ) details of the intended business;
(e ) letters of agreement with associated companies outside
the Republic for applicants intending to deal in
international mail; and
(f) annual reports or audited accounts of the applicant.
(3) The applicant shall not submit the application to the
Courier Service Licensing Committee unless-the application is
made not less than fourteen days before the applicant has
published the intention to apply for the courier service licence in
the Gazette and in the daily issue of a national newspaper.
Statutory lnstrnments ZlstFebruary,2003
62
Courier 10. (1) The Registrar of the Courier Service Licensing
service
Committee shall acknowledge receipt of the application form
licence
and submit the application to the Courier Service Licensing
Committee.
(2) The Courier Service Licensing Committee shall consider
the application and shall communicate its decision to the applicant
not later than sixty days after receipt of an application.
(3) The Courier Service Licensing Committee shall issue a
licence to operate a courier service on payment oft he appropriate
fee set out in the Third Schedule and subject to such conditions
as the Courier Service Licensing Committee may, in consultation
with the Minister, determine.
(4 ) The licence to operate a courier service shall specify the
types ofc ourier service business as follows:
(a) international and national;
(b) international and national (urban)
( c) national, urban and rural; and
(d)rural.
Rural postal 11. For purposes oft hese Regulations, the Courier Service
service
Licensing Committee shall designate areas within the Republic
as rural and urban for the purposes oflicensing courier service
business in such areas:
Provided that the Courier Service Licensing Committee shall
designate the areas listed in the Second Schedule as urban areas.
Suspension 12. (1) Subject to sub-regulation (2) where the Courier
and
cancellation Service Licensing Committee is satisfied that a person issued
of licence
with a licence to operate a courier service is conducting its courier
service operations contrary to the conditions oft he licence issued
under regulation I 0, the Courier Service Licensing Committee
shall-
(i) suspend the licence; or
(ii) cancel the licence.
(2) The Courier Service Licensing Committee shall not
suspend or cancel a licence under this Regulation unless ninety
days prior to the suspension or cancellation a notice has been
served on the person licenced to operate a courier service stating-
21stF ebruary,2 003 Statutory Instruments
63
(a ) the contravention of the condition of the licence
specifying the matters constituting the contravention;
(b) specifying the steps to be taken to remedy the
contravention; and
(c ) specifying the time limit within which the steps described
under paragraph (b) have to be taken.

sections.heading: The Courier Service Licensing Committee shall refuse
sections.text:
Restrictions
to issue a licence to operate a courier service if satisfied that the on issue of
licence
applicant-
(a ) is an undischarged bankrupt or has entered into a
composition or a scheme of arrangement with the
applicant's creditors which is still binding;
(b) has within a period offi ve years immediately preceding
the date oft he application been convicted of an offence
against the Bankruptcy Act; or
Cap.82
(c ) has within a period off ive years immediately preceding
the date of the application, had a licence issued under
these Regulations cancelled.

sections.heading: (1) A person aggrieved by the Courier Service Licensing Appeals
sections.text:
Committee's refusal to register a person, or its declsion to suspend
or cancel a licence, may appeal to the Minister within tlili:ty days
after the decision in form CS3 of the First Schedule.
(2) A person who is not satisfied with the decision of the
Minister under subregulation (I) may appeal to the High Court.
